+1 to strike when thrown at levels 3, 8, and 12.
+2 damage at levels 2 and 8.

entities.name: W.P. Quick Draw
entities.summary:
W.P.: Quick Draw
W.P. QUICK DRAW
This is a practiced move in which the character gets an initiative
bonus to draw and fire or throw their weapons much faster at the first
sign of danger. Note that characters with a high P.P. benefit most from
this skill.
W.P. Bonuses: -
P.P. ≤ 17 = +1 initiative.
P.P. 18-23 = +2 initiative.
P.P. 24-30 = +3 initiative.
P.P. ≥ 31 = +4 initiative.

entities.name: W.P. Rifle
entities.summary:
W.P.: Rifles
W.P. RIFLES
This is familiarity with automatic and semi-automatic rifles, and military assault rifles like the M-16 and AK-47.
These types of weapons are usually capable of Single Shots, Short
Bursts , Long Bursts , Entire Magazine Bursts , and Wild/Spray Fire (if the specific rifle can use such types of magazines or drums).
W.P. Bonuses: +1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, and 13.

entities.name: W.P. Rope
entities.summary:
W.P.: Rope
W.P. ROPE
This W.P. is only available to characters who can select Cowboy Skills.
This is trained use of a lariat or lasso for combat as well as using
the lasso to snare and hook onto a sturdy object or limb for climbing,
scaling walls, boarding vessels, and swinging across openings. As a
weapons, the rope and lasso is used to encircle the shoulders,arms,
waist or feet to snare, trip and otherwise pin and incapacitate an
opponent. The lasso can also ensnare the neck and strangle opponents.
Trip Attacks: A successful roll to strike knocks the victim to
the ground and they lose initiative and one melee attack/action. If the
attacker is on horseback, in a vehicle, or super strong, they may pull
or drag the victim.
Entangle Attacks: A successful roll to strike ensnares the
desired area of the body, typically the arm, upper torso, waist, leg,
or foot. A subsequent successful roll to disarm or entangle may be then
made.
Disarm Attacks: After a successful Entangle Attack, a further
successful roll to strike means the lasso is jerked in such a way that
it slips off the victim's arm, but it takes whatever they were holding
in that hand with it. 60% chance of knocking it to the ground, 61-100%
chance the item is lassoed and can be pulled into the attacker's hand
in two melee actions (four total attacks are used up: one to entangle
the arm, one to disarm the arm, and two to pull the item).
Incapacitate Attacks: After a successful Entangle Attack, a
further successful roll to strike means the attacker can incapacitate
that limb/part of the body (usually by pulling the rope and the lassoed
limb taut), making it impossible for the victim to use it. If an arm or
hand, the victim must use their other arm and hand (at half their usual
bonuses and -1 melee attack unless they are ambidextrous).
If it is the foot or leg that is lassoed and incapacitated (pulled
tight), the victim runs or hops at half speed, can not get away unless
they can cut the rope, is -4 to dodge and roll with impact, and -1 to
parry, disarm, or pull punch. Using s a second melee
attack to jerk the lasso will knock the victim off their feet (losing
initiative and one melee attack)and he can then be pulled/dragged by
hand, horse, or vehicle, or hog-tied (costs the attacker two additional
melee attacks/actions unless the victim can roll a 17 or higher to
dodge the attacker succeeds without further incident. A roll of 17or
higher enables the victim to squirm and move in such a way as to
prevent being tied up and they can then engage the attacker in hand to hand combat).
If it is the upper body that is lassoed, it pins one arm to their body
rendering it useless. The victim must use their other hand (at half
their usual
bonuses and -1 melee attack unless they are ambidextrous). A second
lasso around the upper body (counts as another melee attack) pins both
arms, leaving the victim unable to use their arms or hands. Trying to
run while the arms are pinned is awkward and difficult, reduce the
victim's speed by 20%, they are -2 to dodge and can not parry. No
damage is inflicted, but the victim is physically incapacitated.
Moreover, the lassoed individual can be pulled off their feet and
knocked down (losing initiative and one melee attack/action) and can be
quickly hog-tied by the attacker (counts as four melee attacks) or be
dragged. Only one victim can be toped and pinned or hog-tied at a time.
Multiple lassos can also be used to snare each limb and 'spread-eagle'
the victim. The victim is physically helpless in this position. Only
spell magic or psionics or other special attacks can be used when
spread-eagled, arms pinned or completely hog-tied.
Strangle Attack: Roping the neck can be sued to prevent the
victim from speaking and makes them barely able to breathe (-1 attack
per melee). Tightening the rope (counts as one melee attack) cuts the
air supply off completely and strangles the victim unless they
can cut themselves free or the attacker releases the victim by
loosening and/or removing the lasso from around the neck. Depending on
how long the victim can go without air, the victim may die. For most
characters, dizziness occurs within 2 minutes (victim is -2 attacks per
melee and -3 on all combat bonuses). Within 4 minutes the victim is
rendered unconscious, unless they can hold their breath for an
extraordinarily long time. If still on their feet, reduce their attacks
and combat bonuses by half again. Within 2 or 3 minutes the victim will
lapse into a coma and die within another 1D4 minutes. Note: A
slow hanging kills the victim in half the time. A good character would
never use a strangling attack to kill or hang, or stops short of
causing coma and death. The strangling attack does NOT work on
characters clad in power armour or full conversion cyborgs, or robots.
Against a supernatural being or creature of magic, the attacker must
have greater Supernatural Strength then the victim.
Escaping a Lasso: Escape is possible if the victim is able to
cut themselves free (provided they have a blade weapon or claws), shoot
themselves free with a gun (first shooting the tow-line and then
pulling the lasso from around their neck, counts as two melee
attacks/actions), or pull themselves free if their P.S. is six points
greater than their attacker.
It takes 1D4 melee actions to cut through an S.D.C. lasso with a S.D.C.
knife, one action if an M.D. blade is used or the character has a
Robotic or Supernatural P.S.. It is impossible, however, to draw a
weapon and cut oneself loose if both arms are pinned or while being
dragged. Of course, psionic or magical telekinesis or ectoplasm may be
used to wield the blade, or some other extraordinary measure may be
applicable (eye beam, bionic finger blaster, etc.).
W.P. Rope can not be used to parry. Does not require Rope Skill to use
as a weapon, but when the Cowboy and Saddle Tramp O.C.C.'s do select
both skills they gain an additional bonus of +2 to strike and disarm
when using the lasso against humanoid opponents.
W.P. Bonuses:-
+1 to strike at levels 1, 4, 8, 12, and 15 (when rope/lasso is used in combat or for boarding).
+1 to entangle.
+1 to disarm.

entities.name: W.P. Sharpshooting
entities.summary:
W.P.: Sharpshooting
W.P. SHARPSHOOTING
Sharpshooting is a combination of special expertise and trick
shooting with a particular type of weapon, typically revolvers, pistols
and energy pistols, but sometimes includes rifles. It was first
developed by gunfighters and quickly adopted by the gunslingers and
lawmen of the New West. This skill can be developed by any of the
Men at Arms O.C.C. but is fairly uncommon among most, except for the
Gunfighter, Sheriff and Gunslinger.
W.P. Sharpshooting must be selected for each weapon type or category of
weapon (revolver, pistol, energy pistol, rifle, etc.) in which the
Sharpshooting expertise is desired. This means if the character wants
to be a specialist with revolvers, that's one Sharpshooting selection
at the cost of two O.C.C. Related Skill selections, plus the W.P.
selection (Sharpshooting can be applied to O.C.C. weapon skills but not
Secondary Skills). If Sharpshooting is also desired for W.P. Energy
Pistol, the skill must be selected again (at the cost of two O.C.C.
Related Skills). This process is repeated for each W.P. for which the
added benefits of Sharpshooting is desired. Since Sharpshooting
selection costs the equivalent of two O.C.C. Related Skill selections,
even most Gunslingers and Gunfighters seldom have more than one or two
Sharpshooting specialties - most Men at Arms do not have any.
All bonuses and tricks are only possible with W.P.s to which the
Sharpshooting skill has been added. Thus, a character with
Sharpshooting: Revolver does not get the Sharpshooting bonuses when
firing an energy pistol. The Sharpshooting skill can be added to all
modern guns, from revolvers (six-shooters) to energy rifles, but does
not include W.P. Heavy Military or W.P. Heavy M.D.; i.e. it can not be
applied to rail guns, rocket launchers, mini-missile launchers,
bazookas, artillery weapons, vehicle or robot weapons, hand grenades,
or explosives. Likewise, Sharpshooting can not be applied to weapons
that do not shoot, such as clubs, maces, knives,s swords, axes, whips,
or even spears, bolas, or other weapons that can be thrown.
The Sharpshooting skill can be combined with the W.P. Archery skill,
but is applicable only to the sling, crossbow, and bow and arrow. Only
the Gunfighter, Sheriff, and Gunslinger O.C.C.s automatically get a few
Sharpshooting skills among their O.C.C. skills, but any additional
selections cost them two O.C.C. Related skills like anybody else. This
skill is never available to robots, master psionics, practitioners of
magic, dragons, demons, or any creatures of magic or supernatural
beings. Juicers, Crazies, and Borgs are limited to only one
Sharpshooting skill. Characters can not trade in Secondary Skills for
the Sharpshooting Skill.
W.P. Bonuses: -
All bonuses are in addition to the conventional W.P. bonuses.
Aimed Shot (applicable to both the single shot and the aimed burst shot): +1
to strike with a P.P. 20 and for every additional 5 P.P. points above
20, but the bonuses are only applicable when the appropriate type of
weapon is used. Aimed shots only. No bonuses for shooting Wild.
Bonus Attack: +1 melee attack when using that specific weapon for the entire melee round (add it at the end).
Quick Draw: +1 to initiative at P.P. 18 and for every additional 4 P.P. points, in addition to other initiative bonuses.
Sharpshooter's Called Shot : The Sharpshooter's 'aimed' shot bonuses are NOT applicable when using this special 'called shot'. Instead the sharpshooter
uses the following superior bonuses: +1 to strike with a P.P. 18 and
for every additional 3 points above 18, but the bonuses are only
applicable when the appropriate type of weapon is used and the special
called shot counts as 2 melee attacks/actions. Aimed shots only.

entities.name: W.P. Shield
entities.summary:
W.P.: Shield
W.P. SHIELD
This is combat skill with large and small shields used primarily for
parrying and self defense. A shield can be used in one hand and a
weapon in the other. No bonuses apply when the shield is thrown.
A shield can not be used to block bullets or energy blasts easily. Any
such attempt is done without bonuses (straight dice roll) and with a
penalty as noted below. Blocking thrown knives, spears, arrows, and
other similar weapons also has a penalty and is noted below.
The shield has limited S.D.C. and only really takes damage when used to
block energy blasts or explosions, or is specifically targeted by an
opponent with the intention of destroying or chopping through the
shield. In these cases, subtract 10% of the damage that would normally
be inflicted by a weapon when parried or blocked by the shield. When
all S.D.C. has gone, the shield has too many holes, cracks, and
weaknesses to offer any further protection. The next strike will punch
right through or shatter it and hit the user at full force and thus
full damage.
W.P. Bonuses: -
+1 to parry at levels 1, 3, 7, 10, and 13.
+1 to strike with shield at levels 4, 8, and 12.
W.P. Penalties:-
-8 to parry bullets or energy blasts.
-3 to parry thrown weapons (knives, spears etc.).

entities.name: W.P. Shotgun
entities.summary:
W.P.: Shotgun
W.P. SHOTGUN
A familiarity with the classic double-barrel shotgun and police and military versions of shotguns with ammo-drums.
Shotguns can fire once or twice per melee (double-barrel). It can fire
both rounds simultaneously or one at a time. Buckshot scatters, and
will strike the specific target plus 1D4 others within its blast area
(approximately a 15ft (4.6m) area).
W.P. Bonuses: +1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 6, 10, and 14.

entities.name: W.P. Spear
entities.summary:
W.P.: Spear
W.P. SPEAR
This is combat skill with large and small spears and javelins (the use
of a rifle equipped with a bayonet also falls into this category). Max
throwing range is 150 feet (45.7m).
W.P. Bonuses: -
+1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, and 12.
+1 to parry at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, and 12.
+1 to strike when thrown at levels 3, 6, 10, and 14.

entities.name: W.P. Staff
entities.summary:
W.P.: Staff
W.P. STAFF
This is a combat skill with large and small staves. Staves are not deisnged for throwing.
W.P. Bonuses:-
+1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 7, 10, and 13.
+1 to parry at levels 2, 5, 8, 11, and 14.
+1 to strike then thrown at levels 5, 10, and 15.

entities.name: W.P. Sub-Machinegun
entities.summary:
W.P.: Sub-Machinegun
W.P. SUB-MACHINEGUN
This modern weapon proficiency includes all small arms automatic
weapons like the uzi.
Sub-Machineguns are capable of Short
Bursts , Long Bursts , Entire Magazine Bursts , and Wild/Spray Fire.
W.P. Bonuses: +1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15.

entities.name: W.P. Sword
entities.summary:
W.P.: Sword
W.P. SWORD
This is a combat skill using large and small swords, including fencing
type rapiers, sabres, broadswords, large swords, and short swords. Swords are not designed to be thrown.
W.P. Bonuses: -
+1 to strike at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15.
+1 to strike when thrown at levels 4, 8, and 12.
+1 to parry at levels 2, 4, 7, 10, and 13.

entities.name: W.P. Targeting
entities.summary:
W.P.: Targeting
W.P. TARGETING
This is expertise with thrown and projectile weapons (but not
bows and arrows, crossbows,or guns), such as the sling, slingshot,
boomerangs, shurikens, throwing knives, throwing sticks, axes (small),
spears, and even siege weapons.
Characters who select both W.P. Targeting and another W.P. for a
missile weapon (but not swords, large axes, staves etc.) get the usual
bonuses for that W.P. plus the bonuses from W.P. Targeting. The
character can also throw two small items, like knives, shurikens, or
throwing sticks, simultaneously at the same target.
Awkward items, such as swords, large axes, hammers,clubs, maces, pole
arms, and most other hand-held weapons as well as articles like frying
pans, shovels, toasters, large bricks, furniture, etc. are not designed
to be thrown. Consequently, the average throwing range is a mere 20ft.
(6m) and the character is -1 to strike. An attacker can try to throw
such weapons further, but is -3 to strike for every additional 10 feet
(3m) beyond 20ft. (6m). Characters with augmented/bionic, robotic, or
supernatural P.S. can throw their weight limit the distance their P.S.
allows, but are -1 to strike tossing the ungainly object at one-third
maximum possible distance, -3 to strike at half that distance, and -6
to strike if further than half.
Heavy items, such as those that are half the character's maximum
carrying weight could be thrown one foot (0.3m) per every point of
human P.S.. [Example: a character with P.S. 9 could throw 45 lbs.
(20kg) 9 feet (2.7m), a P.S. of 15 could throw 75lbs. (34kg) 15 feet
(4.6m), or a P.S. 32 could throw 320lbs. (144kg) a distance of 32 feet
(9.7m). Penalties are the same as if an awkward item. Augmented P.S.
have double the range (2ft./0.6m per P.S. point). Robotic P.S. have
triple the range (3ft./0.9m per P.S. point) and have the same penalties
as awkward items.
Supernatural P.S. of 17 or higher can throw half their maximum carrying
weight (P.S. number x25) 5 feet (1.5m) per P.S. point [so a
Supernatural P.S. of 24 can carry 1200lbs. (540kg) and throw half as
much 120ft. (36.6m) with a minimum penalty of -1 to strike.
Supernatural P.S. of 16 or less is the same as for Robotic P.S.. The
penalties are as for awkward items.
The damage bonus for strength does not apply to arrows fired from a
bow, or from a thrown weapon unless the O.C.C. description indicates
otherwise. There are a few very special O.C.C.s that get the damage
bonus for arrows and thrown weapons, but normally the damage bonus is
only for melee combat.
A thrown weapon is considered a ranged attack, however, the P.P.
attribute bonus to strike does apply, and you can do an Aimed or Called
Shot with thrown weapons. A thrown weapon can also be used to Disarm
via an Aimed or Called shot (toss);bonuses to Disarm via Hand to Hand
combat apply.
A natural 19 or 20 does double damage. If the attack was an
Aimed/Called Shot to Disarm, the attack is an automatic success. The
only way for this attack to be parried or dodged by an opponent is for
the defender to match or better the roll with their own natural,
unmodified roll.
Unless stated otherwise, each thrown items counts as one melee
attack/action. Thus, if a character normally has 4 hand to hand attacks
per melee, they can throw four knives or darts per round. Some exotic
weapons may require a spinning action or time to throw that takes up
two or more melee attacks/actions.
Note: The character loses all bonuses and the rate of fire is halved when:-
Running and shooting.
Running and throwing.
Flying.
Riding on horseback
From a moving vehicle
Anytime when unbalanced.

Usage note: this is meant to be paired with an appropriate missile-weapon proficiency (for example a spear or thrown-weapon W.P.), but the exact category rule is broader than the current prerequisite parser can model.

entities.name: W.P. Tomahawk
entities.summary:
W.P.: Tomahawk
W.P. TOMAHAWK
This is training with all types of light throwing axes and tomahawks. Almost exclusive for Native American Indians.
W.P. Bonuses:-
+1 to strike at levels 2, 4, 7, 10, and 13.
+1 to parry at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, and 12.
+1 to strike when thrown at levels 1, 3, 6, 8, 10, and 13.

entities.name: W.P. Whip
entities.summary:
W.P.: Whip
W.P. WHIP
This is skill at 'whipping' or snapping with long, lightweight,
flexible weapons, typically made of leather or reeds. Examples include
Bull Whips and Cat-O-Nine-Tails.
This weapon can not be used to parry and can not be thrown.
W.P. Bonuses: +1 to strike, disarm or entangle at levels 2, 4, 8, and 12.

entities.name: Boat Building
entities.summary:
This skill enables the character to build a variety of rafts, small
rowboats, large flatbed boats, and canoes. A small raft (twp-man) can
be made within 1D6+1 hours if lumber and other suitable materials and
tools are readily available. A large raft will take about 4D6 hours, a
canoe 2D6 days, rowboat 4D4 days, large flatbed1D4x10 days. Time is
doubled or tripled if trees must be cut down and wood prepared. Of
course, the time can be reduced if several knowledgeable people are
working on the construction. A failed skill roll means the boat has
leaks.Trying to patch it will require 4D4 hours.
Base Skill: 25%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Carpentry
entities.summary:
A fundamental knowledge of working with wood. The character knows how
to prepare, treat,preserve and cut wood, recognize quality
craftsmanship, repair damaged wood and wooden items, and build
furniture and articles (chests, boxes, chairs, tables, cabinets,
houses, fences, frames, staves, utensils, etc.).
Base Skill: 25%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Dowsing
entities.summary:
The ability to locate fresh water whether it be with a divining rod or
by more scientific and logical means. This is done through deductive
analysis and knowledge of nature. The percentage indicates the success
ratio of locating fresh water. A can roll can be made (by the DM) once
every melee to sense water, but two consecutive successful rolls are
required to locate the water.
Base Skill: 20%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Fasting
entities.summary:
The ability to go for long periods of time without food. So long as the
character has sufficient water, they can easily survive having no food
for two weeks without physical penalty. Every day after that, the
character must roll under the fastingskill to avoid becoming weakened
or sick.
A failed roll means the following attribute penalties apply:-
I.Q. -1
P.S. -1
P.P. -1
P.E. -2
Spd -3
S.D.C. -3
All these penalties are temporary and will return at the same rate that
they were lost with rest and proper food and water on a daily basis.
The character can also go for two dayswithout water and not show any serious effects from dehydration.
Base Skill: 40%
Per Experience Level: +3%

entities.name: Fishing
entities.summary:
The fundamental methods and enjoyment of the relaxing sport of fishing.
Areas of knowledge include the use of lures,bait, poles, hooks, lines,
and the cleaning and preparation of fish for eating. Also includes a
basic knowledge of freshwater fish, their habits, behaviour, and what
they taste like prepared for eating.
Base Skill: 40%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Horsemanship
entities.summary:
The horsemanship skill for Skilled Equestrians or Knights (other than the Cyber-Knight).
Base Skill: 40%/30%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Horsemanship: Exotic Animals
entities.summary:
The basic riding skill but the character is experienced in riding tame
animals other than the horse. Exotic animals can include: elephants,
camels, lamas, pegasus, gryphons, dinosaurs, giant insects, and other
monstrous beasts trained (or willing) to be ridden. Wild, untamed
creatures can not be ridden, except by Simvan and select other
characters with special animal skills or powers.
Note: Characters with any Horsemanship skill can quickly figure
out how to ride exotic animals and alien riding beasts but at a penalty
of-12% to ride ground/running animals and at -20% to ride flying
animals or tree climbing and leaping animals. Similarly, beings from
other dimensions not familiar with riding fast and sleek Earth horse
are -10% to ride them. Penalty applies to the second percentile for
tricks, stunts, racing, and maintaining control. The horseman knows
nothing about judging quality nor the care or grooming of exotic
animals.
Base Skill: 40%/20%
Per Experience Level: +4%

entities.name: Hunting
entities.summary:
The skill of killing and preparing animals for food. Includes a fair
knowledge of animal habits and patterns,hunting techniques for baiting,
trapping, and the construction of binds and tree stands that conceal
one's presence and increase the chance of getting prey.
Base Skill: None
Per Experience Level: N/A

entities.name: Identify Plants & Fruits
entities.summary:
Training in the recognition of the many different types of wild plants
and vegetation, and where they grow. The emphasis is on finding and
identifying edible berries, fruit, vegetables, mushrooms, roots, bark,
and plants, as well as plants that have herbal and medicinal qualities
(and staying away from poisonous ones). The DM should roll for every 15
(60 melees) minutes of searching to see if the character has located
enough edible food for a decent meal. The DM should roll every 30
minutes (120 melees) to see if the character has located a plant that
could be used as a medicine, disinfectant, or herb.
Base Skill: 25%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Land Navigation
entities.summary:
This skill enables the person to stay on course while travelling over
land by means of observation and memorization. It includes mentally marking/recognizing
geographic landmarks, estimating distance and movement, recognizing
sounds, night travel, marking a trail, and other navigational tricks. The DM should roll
once, for the player, approximately every three miles (4.8km)
travelled. A failed roll means the character is drifting off course:
2D10x100 yards/meters. Consecutive failed rolls means the individual
does not realize that they are off course and continues in the
incorrect direction.
Note: A group of average men can cover about five miles (8km) an
hour at a brisk, but relaxed, pace through grassland, along dirt roads
or pavement. Travel through dense forest or jungle at a cautious pace,
with eyes open for danger, is about 1 mile (1.6km) an hour. Heavy rain
or snow, fog, swamps and other environmental conditions will also
reduce speed to a mile or less per hour. Map reading is done by looking
at symbols (not words) and is -20%. The use of navigational instruments
is not possible.
Base Skill: 36%
Per Experience Level: +4%

entities.name: Preserve Food
entities.summary:
The knowledge in the preparation of foods for storage and later
consumption. Includes canning fresh fruit and vegetables, making jams
and honey, drying fruit, vegetables, and herbs, drying meat and fish,
plus various smoking, salting, and pickling methods.
Base Skill: 30%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Skin & Prepare Animal Hides
entities.summary:
Training in the methods and techniques in skinning, tanning, stretching, and preserving animal hides as fur or leather.
Base Skill: 30%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Spelunking
entities.summary:
The art and practice of exploring underground caves.Spelunking is the
knowledge of caves and cave types and their individual
characteristics.This skill enables the character to navigate a cave or
underground cave network, retain a sense of direction (including up and
down), have a sense of the cave's depth, know how to make subtle
markings and note formations to find one's way outand to better
navigate the caves at a later date, as well as rock climbing. The
spelunker can answer questions about a cave system, such as what it is
called (if it is a named landmark), where it may lead, how deep it
continues, what type of rock it leads through and so on. This skill
also lends familiarity with the correct equipment needed to go
spelunking, and how to apply it. It also includes the knowledge to
recognize and identify various dangers, identifying the flora and fauna
of cave systems and finding and climbing the best routes and direction.
Unskilled characters wandering about in a large series of natural
caverns have about the same chance of survival as unskilled climbers
attempting to scale a major mountain without guidance or equipment.
Base Skill: 35%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Track & Trap Animals
entities.summary:
This skill enables the character to identify an animal by its tracks,
scent, spoor, and habits. The individual can also follow the animal's
tracks and other signs. This means they can estimate how fresh the
tracks are, what direction they are heading, whether the animal is hurt
or sick, and guess its age. The habits and habitats of animals and
animal behaviour are also learned. The first percentile indicates the
character's tracking abilities and the aforementioned knowledge. The
second percentile indicates the character's ability at trapping animals
using and setting snares, clamp traps (like the iron bear trap),pits,
nets and cage traps.
Tracking humans with this skill is also possible, but is done with a
penalty of -20%. Disarming any traps meant for use against humans is
done at half the character's normal skill ability for using animal
traps.
Base Skill: 20%/30%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Wilderness Survival
entities.summary:
Techniques for living off the land, getting water, food, shelter, and help when stranded in
wild forests, deserts, or mountains. Characters without this skill will
not be able to stay healthy for more than a few days in the wilderness
once their supplies run out.
Base Skill: 30%
Per Experience Level: +5%

entities.name: Bio-Regeneration
entities.summary: Self-healing of disease, poison, and physical damage via concentration.
notes:
This is the ability to mentally heal oneself of disease, poison, or physical damage. Unfortunately, this ability cannot be used to help others. The process requires one full minute of concentration, meaning the character cannot use any other psionic power during that time.

Bio-Regeneration does not restore missing limbs, but it does restore 2D6 Hit Points or 3D6 physical S.D.C. points per melee round. In addition, when cuts and scrapes are healed through bio-regeneration there is no scarring. This power can be used as often as once every other minute (every eight melee rounds).

entities.name: Deaden Pain
entities.summary: Negates pain; useful as painkiller or anesthetic.
notes: The ability to deaden pain; can be used as a pain killer which temporarily negates existing pain or as an anesthetic to be used for surgery.

entities.name: Detect Psionics
entities.summary: Detects nearby psionic energy and rough strength.
notes:
This is a mental probe that will indicate the presence of psionic energy within the immediate area. The probe cannot be used to pinpoint the exact location of psychic energy nor the level of power other than a vague sense of weak, medium, or powerful, but will indicate whether an individual is psychic by directing the probe at a specific person (does not reveal the type or strength of the person's powers other than low, medium, high).

It will also indicate whether a Group Mind Block is in effect and also indicate if a person is psionically possessed.
Range: Self; 30 feet per level sensing

entities.name: Exorcism
entities.summary: Psychic rite to expel possessing supernatural entities from living hosts.
notes:
The healer can perform a Rite of Exorcism that uses psychic energy to expel the loathsome being from its mortal host body.

A completely successful exorcism will free the victim of the supernatural force that controls him and send it back to its own non-earthly realm. A partially successful exorcism will drive the evil force out of the possessed person or animal, but does not send the thing back to its own dimension. Thus, it can try to possess the psychic healer or flee to wander in search of a new victim.

An exorcism can only be used on living people and animals whose bodies are inhabited by a supernatural entity or other type of invasive spirit.

Roll to determine success at the end of the rite:
- Expel from host: 28% +7% per level of experience.
- Banish to own dimension: 21% +7% per level of experience.

During the exorcism, the possessing force can use powers from the host and itself, but the rite weakens it, reducing attacks per melee by half.

entities.name: Healing Touch
entities.summary: Instantly restores HP/SDC to another living creature.
notes:
The Healing Touch is a remarkable healing ability that can instantly heal cuts, burns, bruises and similar physical wounds. The touch restores 1D6 (or 2D4) Hit Points or 2D6 S.D.C.

The Healing Touch can only be used on other living creatures, never on the psychic himself.

entities.name: Increased Healing
entities.summary: Temporarily doubles natural/professional healing rates.
notes: The healer is able to stimulate another person's healing energies, dramatically increasing recuperative powers. Hit Points and S.D.C. recovery are double normal professional treatment rates for the duration.

entities.name: Induce Sleep
entities.summary: Induces normal restful sleep; unwilling victims get save bonuses.
notes:
This is not an offensive ability, but is intended to be a recuperative power to induce sleep on those who are ill, exhausted, or an insomniac.

The person falls into a normal, restful sleep from which they can be easily roused. Unwilling victims get a +5 bonus to save vs psychic attack.
Duration: One hour per level (or until awakened)

entities.name: Psychic Diagnosis
entities.summary: Pinpoint diagnosis of injury, disease, and possession.
notes:
The psychic healer can sense physical pain and damage, external and internal injury, disease and possession with absolute clarity.

This pinpoint accuracy enables the character to suggest treatment or to conduct psychic surgery.

entities.name: Psychic Purification
entities.summary: Neutralizes drugs, poisons, and toxins in the target.
notes:
The healer can use this ability to slow a person's metabolism and destroy chemicals, drugs, poisons, and toxins in the body.

Effects are immediately halted at the beginning of meditation and completely neutralized by the end. Damage sustained prior to intervention remains.

entities.name: Psychic Surgery
entities.summary: Performs surgery with bare hands using psychic force.
notes:
Psychic Surgery is used to repair broken bones, internal injuries, and remove foreign objects (bullets, arrows, etc.) using only bare hands; no tools or instruments.

It can also be used to help heal a character who has lapsed into a coma from severe injury. Psychic Surgery is equivalent to professional hospital treatment, with minimal bleeding, reduced pain, and no scarring. A psychic diagnosis must be made before surgery is possible.

entities.name: Resist Fatigue
entities.summary: Temporarily suppresses fatigue and exhaustion.
notes:
A mind-over-matter discipline which enables the character to engage in physical activity without suffering exhaustion for the duration.

However, fatigue is only suspended, so the psychic feels extremely tired and may collapse when the power ends.
Duration: One hour + 20 minutes per level

entities.name: Restore P.P.E.
entities.summary: Converts ISP into PPE and transfers it to another person.
notes:
This power allows the psychic to convert I.S.P. into P.P.E. and transfer that energy to another person.

Conversion: 2 I.S.P. = 1 P.P.E., plus 4 I.S.P. to initiate the conversion/transfer.
Example: 10 I.S.P. converts to 5 P.P.E., and costs 4 extra I.S.P. to transfer (14 I.S.P. total).

entities.name: Suppress Fear
entities.summary: Suppresses fear responses; auto-succeeds against Horror Factor while active.
notes:
This power temporarily suppresses the chemical and psychological components of fear in the recipient.

While active, the character can think calmly, take deliberate action, and automatically succeeds on Horror Factor checks (including magically induced fear). It can be used on self or others by touch.

entities.name: Cloud of Smoke

entities.name: Death Trance

entities.name: Globe of Daylight

entities.name: See Aura
entities.summary: Reveals experience level (low/med/high), presence of magic, psychic abilities, high/low PPE, possession, and non-human nature. Mind block masks psionic/PPE info.

entities.name: See the Invisible

entities.name: Sense Evil
entities.summary: Sense supernatural evil in area. Indicates approximate number (one/few/several/many), intensity, and general location. Human evil only detected with immediate harmful intent.

entities.name: Sense Magic
entities.summary: Sense the presence of magic like a Geiger counter — near (within 20ft) or far. Detect enchanted objects, active spells, or magic being used in range.

entities.name: Thunderclap
entities.summary: Booming thunderclap stuns and distracts everyone within 30ft (except caster). Grants +5 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, and creates HF 8 for all nearby.

entities.name: Befuddle
entities.summary: Causes one victim to become confused and disoriented. −2 to strike/parry/dodge, attacks halved, all skills −20%.

entities.name: Chameleon
entities.summary: Blend into surroundings. 90% undetectable if still, 70% moving slowly (≤2ft/melee), 20% moving moderately (≤6ft/melee), 0% if faster.

entities.name: Climb
entities.summary: Climb any normal surface at 98% skill (speed = Spd attribute). Smooth/difficult surfaces at 60%. Includes rappelling.

entities.name: Concealment

entities.name: Detect Concealment

entities.name: Extinguish Fire

entities.name: Fear

entities.name: Heavy Breathing

entities.name: Levitation

entities.name: Mystic Alarm

entities.name: Float in Air

entities.name: Fuel Flame

entities.name: Ignite Fire

entities.name: Impervious to Poison

entities.name: Negate Poison/Toxin

entities.name: Astral Projection

entities.name: Blind

entities.name: Carpet of Adhesion
entities.summary: Creates a sticky carpet (max 10×20ft) up to 90ft away. Victims are stuck for full duration. Save: 2d6 melees to pull free. Effective against borgs, power armor, and robots.

entities.name: Charismatic Aura

entities.name: Cure Minor Disorders

entities.name: Energy Field

entities.name: Fool's Gold

entities.name: Fire Bolt

entities.name: Magic Net

entities.name: Multiple Image

entities.name: Repel Animals

entities.name: Seal

entities.name: Swim as a Fish

entities.name: Domination

entities.name: Energy Disruption

entities.name: Escape

entities.summary: Illusory mask over caster's face. Imitate a specific person at 20% + 5%/level (or use Disguise base). Viewers save vs magic at −4 to see through it.

entities.name: Memory Bank
entities.summary: Implant up to 1000 words of data into another person without their knowledge. Retrieve with a touch. Mind block prevents both implanting and retrieval.

entities.name: Reduce Self (6 inches)
entities.summary: Instantly shrinks caster to 6 inches tall. MDC weapons do only 1 point of damage. Others just sting.

entities.name: Teleport: Lesser
entities.summary: Teleport up to 50 lbs of non-living matter to a known location. Success ratio: 80% + 2%/level. Failure means object ends up somewhere else within range.

entities.name: Time Slip
entities.summary: Slip 7 seconds into the future. Move freely while others are frozen. Cannot harm living beings. All others lose 2 attacks that melee. Ideal for escape.

entities.name: Tongues
entities.summary: Perfectly understand and speak ALL spoken languages at 98%. Does not include written languages (see Eyes of Thoth).

entities.name: Words of Truth
entities.summary: Target must answer questions truthfully. Caster can ask 2 brief questions per melee. Target saves for each question — success means no answer required.

entities.name: Constrain Being
entities.summary: Forces a lesser supernatural creature to obey simple orders ("back", "stop", "go"). If attacked, enchantment breaks. Does not affect greater supernaturals or non-supernatural D-Bees.

entities.name: Agony
entities.summary: Incapacitates victim with excruciating pain. No attacks, no movement, no speech. One minute recovery: attacks/speed halved, −1 to strike/parry/dodge. No physical damage.

entities.name: Animate and Control Dead
entities.summary: Animate 2 + 1/level corpses/skeletons as puppets. Must remain in line of sight. Speed 7, 2 attacks/melee, 1d6 damage. Only total destruction stops them.

entities.name: Dispel Magic Barriers
entities.summary: Negates all magic barriers — carpets of adhesion, wall spells, wards, seals, etc. The barrier being attacked gets a standard save (12) to resist.

entities.name: Fly as the Eagle
entities.summary: Full flight at max 50mph. +1 to parry, +2 to dodge, +2 damage on diving attack. Bonuses only while in flight.

entities.name: Globe of Silence
entities.summary: Invisible 10ft radius globe absorbs all sound — nothing enters or leaves. Spell casters inside cannot cast (spoken words unheard). Globe can be moved or fixed in place.

entities.name: Invisibility: Superior
entities.summary: Invisible to ALL detection — infrared, heat, motion, smell, nightvision, animals. No footprints, Prowl 84%. Broken only by hostile action (accidentally bumping someone is NOT hostile).

entities.name: Invulnerability
entities.summary: Impervious to fire, energy, poisons/toxins/gases. 35 MDC glowing force field. +10 to save vs magic, psionic assault, and horror factor. When MDC depleted, normal damage resumes.

entities.name: Life Drain
entities.summary: SDC/HP/speed halved, attacks −1, skills −10%. After spell: skills/attacks recover immediately, SDC at 8/hour, HP at 4/hour, weakness remains 6 hours. At level 4+ affects 15ft area.

entities.name: Metamorphosis: Animal
entities.summary: Completely transform into any animal. Retains own IQ, speech, memory, SDC, and HP while gaining all animal abilities. Return to human form (naked) at will.

entities.name: Purification
entities.summary: Purifies food, water, or air of natural contaminants, poisons, and toxins. Cannot affect magical or psionic contamination.

entities.name: Commune with Spirits
entities.summary: Lets the caster see and speak with entities of all types, including poltergeists, trapped entities, and possessors. It does not compel obedience.

entities.name: Exorcism
entities.summary: Forces a possessing entity out of its host. The entity gets two immediate possession attempts on nearby targets; if both fail, it is banished or driven off for months.

entities.name: Eyes of the Wolf
entities.summary: Grants nightvision 60ft, 75% see the invisible, and boosted wilderness detection skills including track, identify tracks, and recognize poison.

entities.name: Hallucination
entities.summary: Implants a private illusion into one victim’s mind. Only that person perceives it, but it feels completely real until the spell ends or they save.

entities.name: Locate
entities.summary: Reveals the general location or environment of a known person or target, such as a building, district, or conveyance rather than exact coordinates.

entities.name: Luck Curse
entities.summary: Inflicts pervasive bad luck: normal combat bonuses drop to zero, skills suffer situational penalties, prowl becomes clumsy, and critical advantages collapse.

entities.name: Metamorphosis: Human
entities.summary: Reshapes the body into any human appearance, altering age, sex, size, skin, hair, and features. It changes appearance only, not voice, skills, or attributes.

entities.name: Minor Curse
entities.summary: Inflicts one persistent nuisance affliction such as fever, nausea, headache, rash, vertigo, or similar minor but disruptive magical disorders.

entities.name: Negate Magic
entities.summary: Attempts to cancel most active spell effects immediately. Success depends on beating the target magic’s save; some categories such as wards and enchantments are exempt.

entities.name: Oracle
entities.summary: The magical equivalent of clairvoyance: grants a dream-like glimpse of a possible future tied to the person, place, or event the caster is contemplating.

entities.name: Sickness
entities.summary: Afflicts the target with severe disease-like symptoms without a true disease. Attacks drop to one, PE plummets, combat penalties apply, and skills fall by 40%.

entities.name: Spoil
entities.summary: Ruins up to 50 lbs of food or 10 gallons of fluid, making it foul and potentially stomach-turning to anyone who consumes it.

entities.name: Stone to Flesh
entities.summary: Transforms stone into flesh and can restore petrified victims. Affects up to 50 lbs of stone per level.

entities.name: Wisps of Confusion
entities.summary: Confuses 1d8 victims, inflicting −5 to strike, parry, and dodge and cutting attacks per melee in half.

entities.name: Curse: Phobia
entities.summary: Implants a specific magical phobia. The victim suffers full phobic reactions whenever confronted with the cursed trigger until cured or the duration ends.

entities.name: Familiar Link
entities.summary: Creates a permanent empathic and sensory bond with a small animal familiar. Damage to the familiar echoes to the mage, and death of the familiar permanently costs hit points.

entities.name: Metamorphosis: Insect
entities.summary: Transforms the caster into a tiny insect or spider while retaining intellect and personal durability. Human skills and spellcasting are impossible in insect form.

entities.name: Mute
entities.summary: Temporarily suppresses speech and vocal sound by affecting the target’s voice box and vocal cords.

entities.name: Protection Circle: Simple
entities.summary: A physically drawn circle that keeps lesser supernatural beings 5ft away and grants those inside +2 to save vs magic and psychic attack.

entities.name: Summon & Control Canines
entities.summary: Summons 1d4 canines plus one per level and gives the caster command over them, along with other nearby canines, while the caster remains in the pentacle.

entities.name: Speed of the Snail
entities.summary: Slows 1d6 targets to one-third normal speed and reduces attacks, dodge, and parry to one-third. It can affect robots and vehicles as well.

entities.name: Transferal
entities.summary: Transfers all but 4 of the caster’s PPE and apparent magical potency into another person temporarily, hiding the caster’s real magical strength.

entities.name: Water to Wine
entities.summary: Transforms up to 10 gallons of fresh water per level into fair-to-average wine, with quality improving by caster level.

entities.name: Banishment
entities.summary: Forces one lesser supernatural being per caster level to leave a 600ft area and prevents return for weeks unless they successfully save.

entities.name: Control/Enslave Entity
entities.summary: Attempts to bind and command up to two entities per caster level. Each entity gets its own save; uncontrolled ones remain dangerous.

entities.name: Metamorphosis: Superior
entities.summary: Transforms the target into any real living creature or the outward form of a supernatural creature. The caster keeps their own mind and personal capabilities.

entities.name: Mystic Portal
entities.summary: Opens a one-way portal that can pass through solid barriers or act as a short-range teleport gateway. Once crossed, the portal vanishes behind the traveler.

entities.name: Summon and Control Rodents
entities.summary: Summons an obedient swarm of rodents and lets the caster command other nearby rodents while inside the ritual pentagram.

entities.name: Summon Shadow Beast
entities.summary: Summons a powerful extradimensional predator that excels in darkness and shadow. There is a real chance it remains behind and turns feral after service ends.

entities.name: Wards
entities.summary: Places visible trigger-glyph traps on objects or surfaces. Supported ward effects include alarm, fear, fire bolt, paralysis, sleep, agony, curses, and banishment.

entities.name: Anti-Magic Cloud
entities.summary: Creates a vast cloud that suppresses nearly all magic within it. Most casters and enchanted items become inert unless they make an exceptional save.

entities.name: Create Mummy
entities.summary: Necromantic ritual that creates a near-indestructible undead guardian. Most attacks are useless against it; fire is the key weakness.

entities.name: Create Magic Scroll
entities.summary: Transfers a known spell into written form so anyone literate in the script can cast it by reading it aloud once, after which the text vanishes.

entities.name: Remove Curse
entities.summary: Attempts to break any curse immediately. Success depends on a boosted save roll; if it fails, the curse remains and the caster must try again.

entities.name: Summon and Control Animals
entities.summary: A superior summoning pentacle that calls and controls broad classes of animals, from tiny swarms to large and exotic beasts.

entities.name: Summon Fog
entities.summary: Calls forth dense fog over a massive area, crippling visibility, slowing travel, and heavily penalizing long-range combat.

entities.name: Amulet
entities.summary: Creates a persistent protective charm. Depending on design, it can boost saves, ward the undead, reveal spirits, or grant see the invisible.

entities.name: Calm Storms
entities.summary: Reduces or suppresses natural and magical storms, weakening rain, wind, waves, and even tornadoes depending on whether it is cast as spell or ritual.

entities.name: Create Zombie
entities.summary: Ritually creates a durable undead servant with limited intelligence. It can obey orders and use simple tools, but only energy, silver, fire, or proper destruction truly stop it.

entities.name: Metamorphosis: Mist
entities.summary: Transforms the target into semitransparent mist, immune to most attacks and able to pass through cracks, though unable to speak or cast spells in that form.

entities.name: Summon and Control Entity
entities.summary: Ritually summons an entity from another dimension and places it under the caster’s direct control for the spell’s duration.

entities.name: Summon and Control Rain
entities.summary: Creates and controls rainfall from drizzle to downpour across a massive area, impairing visibility and movement.
entities.range: Immediate area or up to 10 miles away/level

entities.name: Time Hole
entities.summary: Places the caster into a personal limbo pocket where time passes faster inside than outside, allowing extended rest or planning while little time elapses in the real world.

entities.name: Create Golem
entities.summary: Ritually creates a stone or iron golem: a resilient magic construct that obeys only its maker and shrugs off most attacks while regenerating unless properly disabled.

entities.name: Protection Circle: Superior
entities.summary: A stronger warding circle that keeps even greater supernatural beings at bay, grants major save bonuses, blocks possession, and restores extra PPE/ISP to those inside.

entities.name: Summon and Control Storm
entities.summary: Creates destructive rainstorms or windstorms capable of flooding, crippling travel, and causing widespread environmental chaos.
entities.range: Immediate area or up to 10 miles away

entities.name: Summon Lesser Being
entities.summary: Ritually summons a lesser supernatural being and binds it to service. When control ends, the creature may remain in the world and become a new problem.

entities.name: Sanctum
entities.summary: Consecrates a room into a magical safe haven hidden from several divinations and inaccessible to undead, with limited resistance even to lesser monsters.
entities.duration: Lifetime of caster or until canceled

entities.name: Talisman
entities.summary: Imbues an object with either a rechargeable stored spell (levels 1–8, excluding illusions) or a rechargeable PPE battery.

entities.name: Close Rift
entities.summary: Attempts to forcibly close a dimensional rift. Each attempt permanently costs the caster 2 PPE from their base, whether it succeeds or fails.

entities.name: Id Barrier
entities.summary: Raises a terrifying energy barrier that repels the weak-willed and forces others to confront an apparition of their greatest fear if they fail to pass through it.

entities.name: Impenetrable Wall of Force
entities.summary: Creates a large shimmering wall that no creature, weapon, or object can pass through except by specially dispelling or negating the magic.

entities.name: Restoration
entities.summary: A major healing spell that fully restores SDC and hit points, heals internal injuries, mends bones, and can restore severed limbs if recovered in time.

entities.summary: Coalition longarm with burst and sustained fire modes.
